verse_text_iast: avyakto ’yam acintyo ’yamavikāryo ’yam ucyatetasmād evaṁ viditvainaṁnānuśocitum arhasi
synonyms: avyaktaḥ — invisible; ayam — this soul; acintyaḥ — inconceivable; ayam — this soul; avikāryaḥ — unchangeable; ayam — this soul; ucyate — is said; tasmāt — therefore; evam — like this; viditvā — knowing it well; enam — this soul; na — do not; anuśocitum — to lament; arhasi — you deserve.
translation: It is said that the soul is invisible, inconceivable and immutable. Knowing this, you should not grieve for the body.
purport_excerpt: As described previously, the magnitude of the soul is so small for our material calculation that he cannot be seen even by the most powerful microscope; therefore, he is invisible. As far as the soul’s existence is concerned, no one can establish his existence experimentally beyond the proof of śruti, or Vedic wisdom.
